skill.name: chart-style-unifier
skill.description: Batch-unify typography (font family, size, italics) for Word table cells and embedded charts; use when you need consistent formatting across theses/reports without changing body text.
skill.summary: Batch-unify typography for Word table cells and embedded charts. Use for consistent formatting across theses and reports without changing body text.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: The static scanner detected 97 potential issues across 7 files (1419 lines). After manual evaluation, all 97 findings are dismissed as false positives. The subprocess.run calls use hardcoded pip install arguments with no user-controlled input. The reported path traversal and weak cryptography findings reference documentation text, not executable code. The env_access findings are Python dict .get() calls on a config object, not environment variable reads. This skill provides legitimate document formatting functionality with no evidence of malicious intent or data exfiltration.

dangerous_patterns.title: Automatic Dependency Installation via Subprocess
dangerous_patterns.description: Script auto-installs missing Python packages using subprocess.run with hardcoded pip install arguments. The package names (python-docx, pywin32) are hardcoded strings, not user input, so command injection risk is minimal. This is a convenience feature but introduces supply-chain risk if PyPI packages are compromised.

dangerous_patterns.title: In-place Document File Modification
dangerous_patterns.description: Script modifies the original .docx file in place rather than creating a copy. The original file path from config.json is used for both reading and saving, which can result in data loss if the operation is interrupted or produces unexpected results.

content.user_title: Unify Chart and Table Fonts in Word Documents
content.value_statement: Researchers and students spend hours manually fixing inconsistent fonts across tables and charts in Word documents. This skill automates batch typography unification so your thesis or report has uniform fonts throughout without changing body text.
actual_capabilities: Change font family and size for all text inside Word table cells across an entire document
Apply italics selectively to numbers, Chinese characters, or English letters inside table cells
Normalize typography for embedded Word chart elements including title, axis labels, legend, tick labels, and data labels
Generate isolated run directories with timestamp-based tracking for reproducible formatting workflows
Auto-install missing Python dependencies (python-docx, pywin32) when not already available
Provide VBA macro code for manual chart normalization in both Word and Excel
limitations: Does not modify body text outside table cells or non-table document content
Chart font normalization requires Windows with Microsoft Word installed for COM automation via pywin32
Cannot process linked charts from Excel source files directly; those must be normalized at the Excel source level
Supports .docx format only; legacy .doc files are not compatible

use_cases.title: Thesis Table Formatting for Graduate Students
use_cases.description: A graduate student finalizing their thesis needs all 50+ tables to use Times New Roman 11pt. The skill batch-processes the entire document in one pass.

use_cases.title: Report Standardization for Research Teams
use_cases.description: A multi-author research report has inconsistent chart fonts from different contributors. The skill normalizes all embedded chart typography to match journal submission guidelines.

use_cases.title: Manuscript Formatting for Publication Editors
use_cases.description: An editor receives manuscripts with mixed table and chart formatting. The skill applies consistent font rules across all documents before production.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Table Font Change
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the chart-style-unifier skill to change the font of all tables in my thesis document to Times New Roman, size 11 point. The file is at C:/Users/me/Documents/thesis.docx.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user needs to change table fonts in a single document

prompt_templates.title: Table Font with Italic Numbers
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the chart-style-unifier skill. Set the table font in my document at /home/user/report.docx to Arial 10pt. Also make all numbers in tables italic.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs font change plus numeric italic formatting

prompt_templates.title: Chart Font Normalization
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the chart-style-unifier skill to normalize chart fonts in my Word document. Set chart title to 11pt bold, axis labels to 9pt, and all other chart text to 9pt. Font family: Calibri.
prompt_templates.scenario: User with embedded Word charts needs consistent chart typography

prompt_templates.title: Full Document Formatting Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the chart-style-unifier skill. First, initialize a run directory. Then configure: target document path is D:/manuscripts/paper.docx, font is SimSun 10pt for tables, and enable Chinese character italics. Execute the table processing. Finally, run chart normalization for embedded charts with the same font.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user executing a multi-step formatting pipeline

output_examples.input: Change all table fonts in my thesis.docx to Times New Roman 11pt.
output_examples.output: Modified 15 tables in total. Modified approximately 234 cells. Font set to Times New Roman at 11.0pt. Document saved successfully.

output_examples.input: Make all numbers in my report tables italic.
output_examples.output: Italicization complete. Processed 8 tables across the document. 42 paragraphs containing numbers have been set to italic. Non-numeric content was not affected.

output_examples.input: Normalize chart fonts in the document to Calibri 9pt with title at 11pt.
output_examples.output: Chart font normalization complete. Modified 12 embedded charts. Updated chart area, titles, axis labels, tick labels, legends, and data labels to Calibri. Note: Chart processing requires Windows with Microsoft Word installed.

best_practices: Always run init_run.py first to create an isolated run directory with a timestamp before executing change_table_font.py. This prevents configuration conflicts between runs.
Make a backup copy of your document before running the script since it modifies the original file in place and the changes cannot be undone automatically.
For documents with linked Excel charts, normalize font styles in the Excel source files first using the provided VBA code, then refresh the links in Word to preserve the new formatting.
anti_patterns: Do not skip the init_run.py initialization step. Running change_table_font.py directly without creating a run directory and config.json will fail with a missing configuration error.
Do not use this skill on documents in protected system directories or with parent traversal paths like ../ in the file path. The validate_doc_path function requires valid existing .docx files.
Do not expect this skill to work on macOS or Linux for chart operations. Chart font normalization requires Windows with Microsoft Word and the pywin32 library for COM automation.

faq.question: Does this skill change body text outside tables?
faq.answer: No. Only text inside table cells is modified. Body text, headers, footers, and other non-table content is not affected.

faq.question: Can this skill process charts on a Mac?
faq.answer: No. Chart font normalization requires Windows with Microsoft Word and the pywin32 library installed. Table font operations do work cross-platform.

faq.question: What Python version is needed?
faq.answer: Python 3.10 or higher is required. The skill also depends on python-docx >= 0.8.11 and optionally pywin32 >= 306 for chart operations.

faq.question: Will this skill modify my original document?
faq.answer: Yes. The script saves changes directly to the document path you provide in config.json. Always make a backup copy before processing.

faq.question: Can this skill handle linked charts from Excel?
faq.answer: The skill cannot process linked charts directly. It provides VBA macro code to normalize font styles in the Excel source files first, after which you refresh the links in Word.

faq.question: What happens if python-docx is not installed?
faq.answer: The script attempts to install it automatically using pip install python-docx. If the installation fails, the script provides instructions for manual installation.
